title: Is your website ready for AI agents?
description: As AI agents begin automating web interactions, websites need to be redesigned for machine readability, not just human users. This article explores how accessibility standards and semantic HTML become critical tools for AI-agent compatibility, reshaping design priorities for the AI era.

title: Designing for AI means designing like it's 1999
description: This thought-provoking essay argues that designing for AI requires returning to web fundamentals—semantic HTML, clean structure, and human-readable content—principles we abandoned during the JavaScript boom. Understanding this shift is essential for designers adapting to AI-first product thinking.

title: pdf.to.design: static PDF → editable Figma design
description: A new tool that converts PDFs into fully editable Figma designs with extracted layers, images, and text processed locally for privacy. This bridges a significant workflow gap for designers who need to digitize static documents into interactive design files.

title: A designer's guide to opening Xcode for the first time
description: A practical guide demystifying Xcode for designers, showing how to leverage Claude Code and MCPs to build functional iOS apps without deep programming knowledge. Empowers designers to prototype and ship native mobile experiences directly.

title: Who is your content actually for?
description: With AI and SEO converging, content creation is increasingly optimized for machines rather than humans, fundamentally shifting what gets written and read. This critical analysis examines how these pressures are deprioritizing actual user value and experience.

title: The user is visibly frustrated
description: An insightful exploration of how AI agents exploit our social instincts while lacking accountability, learning, or adaptation. Understanding this gap between perceived and actual AI capabilities is crucial for designing honest, responsible AI interactions.

title: AI Token Scarcity and Arcade Economics
description: As computing costs for AI rise, designers must rethink what's feasible and sustainable in their workflows and products. This economic reality check explores how token scarcity will reshape design practices and tool expectations.

title: Design Systems News - Issue 217 - 1st Jun 2026
description: The latest curated collection of design systems articles, including best practices for linting design tokens to ensure consistency and prevent errors. Essential reading for design leaders managing scalable, maintainable design infrastructure.
